hoodie.name: hoodie
hoodie.look: Field uniform of the remote professional. Battle-tested against thermostat disputes.

mug.name: coffee mug
mug.look: Your emotional-support mug. Currently empty, like the status dashboard's promises.

look.text: Your emotional-support mug, now bearing actual diner coffee. Steam rises off it like uptime returning.
Your emotional-support mug. Currently empty, like the status dashboard's promises.

do.narrate: You drink Darlene's coffee. Diner-grade: brewed strong enough to route packets on its own. Morale patch applied successfully.

use.text: You tip the empty mug back anyway. One heroic drop reports in. It tastes like Tuesday.

cable.name: ethernet cable
cable.look: Fourteen feet of cat 5 you crimped by hand, back when you trusted nothing. Both ends test clean. It may be the most reliable object in the hemisphere right now.

edge_key.name: janitor's key
edge_key.look: Dale's spare: brass, unremarkable, nine years patient inside a lockbox. The most important physical object in the western internet, and it cost $2.40 to duplicate. It opens exactly one gray door, and the gray door knows it's coming.

coat.name: coat
coat.look: The Going Outside coat, over your arm. It smells faintly of the previous decade, which is when it last worked a full shift.

look.text: The Going Outside coat, on you and on duty. Its one job is broadcasting that you have somewhere to be, and business is booming.

use.text: The coat is already on and already at its post. Stacking a second shift onto it would void whatever warranty the decade left.

do.narrate: You shrug on the Going Outside coat. It survived the decade better than most contracts. You are now dressed for elsewhere — which, today, is everywhere. The coat leaves your inventory and assumes its post.

pants.name: real pants
pants.look: Real Pants: rigid, buttoned, unforgiving. Last worn at a funeral for a startup. They still hold the crease. Some things stay load-bearing.

look.text: You are in the Real Pants right now. The crease precedes you into rooms by a full second. Witnesses adjust their posture.

use.text: You are already wearing the Real Pants. Their authority cannot be stacked.

do.narrate: You put on the Real Pants. The crease engages. Your posture files a change request and approves it unilaterally. Somewhere, a conference room feels a disturbance. You are dressed for a service call. The pants leave your inventory and enter your identity.

take.text: You're wearing them. Further acquisition would be a laundry incident.
